seoTitle: How to Delete a Deposited Payment in QuickBooks Online
metaDescription: Learn how to delete a received payment that's already been deposited in QuickBooks Online without throwing off your reconciled bank records.
intro: Deleting a payment that's already been swept into a deposit takes a bit more care than removing a standalone transaction. This guide explains the steps to correctly delete a deposited payment in QuickBooks Online.

steps.heading: Locate the deposit
steps.body: Find the deposit transaction that includes the payment you need to remove.

steps.heading: Review the deposit's contents
steps.body: Check which individual payments make up that deposit before making any changes.

steps.heading: Remove the payment from the deposit
steps.body: Edit the deposit to take out the specific payment, or delete the payment itself if the deposit needs to be undone first.

steps.heading: Re-reconcile if necessary
steps.body: If the deposit was already reconciled, review your reconciliation afterward to make sure everything still matches your bank statement.

faq.question: Will deleting a payment affect my bank reconciliation?
faq.answer: Yes, if the deposit was already reconciled, removing a payment from it can throw off the reconciled balance, so review it afterward.

faq.question: Can I just delete the whole deposit instead?
faq.answer: You can, but that also removes any other payments bundled into that same deposit, so it's often better to edit rather than delete entirely.

faq.question: What happens to the invoice after I delete the payment?
faq.answer: The related invoice will reopen and show as unpaid or partially paid once the payment is removed.

faq.question: Is there a way to avoid needing to delete deposited payments?
faq.answer: Reviewing payments before including them in a deposit can help catch errors early, before reconciliation makes changes more complex.
